beak.experimental.io module

beak.experimental.io.check_path(folder: str | Path)[source]

Check if path exists and create if not.

Parameters:

folder (Path) – The path to check and create if necessary.

Returns:

The input path as Path object.

beak.experimental.io.compress_to_zip(file_path: Path, method: int = 14, level: int = 5)[source]

Compresses a file to a zip archive in the same location.

Parameters:

file_path (Path) – The path to the file to be compressed.

Returns:

None

beak.experimental.io.copy_files(file_paths: Sequence[Path], old_base_path: Path, new_base_path: Path, keep_folder_structure: bool = True)[source]

Copies files from a list of paths to a new location, maintaining their relative folder structure.

Parameters:

  • file_paths – A list of absolute file paths.

  • old_base_path – The directory to replace in the original paths.

  • new_base_path – The new base directory to save the files to.

  • keep_folder_structure – Whether to keep the original folder structure (default: True)

Returns:

None

beak.experimental.io.copy_folder_structure(source_folder: Path, destination_folder: Path, include_source=True, verbose: int = 0)[source]

Copy folder structure from source folder to destination folder.

Parameters:

  • source_folder (Path) – The source folder.

  • destination_folder (Path) – The destination folder.

  • include_source (bool) – Whether to include the source folder. Defaults to True.

  • verbose (int, optional) – The verbosity level. Defaults to 1.

beak.experimental.io.create_file_folder_list(root_folder: Path, exclude_types: Sequence[str] | None = None) → tuple[List[Path], List[Path]][source]

Create a list of files and folders in a root folder (including subfolders).

Parameters:

  • root_folder (Path) – The root folder to search for folders.

  • exclude_types (Optional[Sequence[str]]) – List of file extensions to exclude.

Returns:

A tuple containing two lists: folders and files.

Return type:

Tuple[List[Path], List[Path]]

beak.experimental.io.create_file_list(folder: str | Path, extensions: Sequence[str] | None = None, recursive: bool = False) → List[Path][source]

Create a list of files in the specified folder with the given extensions.

Parameters:

  • folder (Path) – The folder path to search for files.

  • extensions (Optional[Sequence[str]]) – Sequence of file extensions to include. Defaults are [“.tif”, “.tiff”].

  • recursive (bool) – Whether to search for files recursively in subdirectories. Defaults to False.

Returns:

A list of Path objects representing the files found.

Return type:

List[Path]

beak.experimental.io.create_model_setup(method: str, input_config: str = 'config_base.json', train_config: str | None = None, base_path: str | None = None, work_dir: str | None = None) → Tuple[str, List, str, str][source]

Create a model setup based on the provided method.

Parameters:

  • method (str) – The method for creating the model setup.

  • input_config (str, optional) – The input configuration file. Defaults to “config_base.json”.

  • train_config (str, optional) – The training configuration file. Defaults to None.

  • base_path (str, optional) – The base path for the processed model data. Defaults to None.

  • work_dir (str, optional) – The working directory. Defaults to the current directory.

Returns:

The model setup dictionary, file list, labels and output path.

beak.experimental.io.create_raster_object_in_memory(array: ndarray, meta: dict)[source]

Create a raster object in memory using the given array and metadata.

Metadata must have the same number of bands as the array’s shape. Do not forget to close the in-memory raster after use with raster.close()

Parameters:

  • array (np.ndarray) – The raster data as a NumPy array.

  • meta (dict) – The metadata of the raster.

Returns:

The raster object in memory.

Return type:

rasterio.io.MemoryFile

beak.experimental.io.data_folder(package_name: str = 'beak', folder_name: str = 'data') → Any[source]

Returns the path to the specified data folder.

If the importlib_resources module is available (Python 3.9 and above), it is used to access the data folder. If importlib_resources is not available, the importlib.resources module is used instead.

Parameters:

folder_name (str) – The name of the data folder. Default is “beak.data”.

Returns:

The relative path to the specified data folder.

Return type:

Path

beak.experimental.io.dataframe_to_feather(data: DataFrame, file_path: Path)[source]

Convert dataframe to feather format.

Parameters:

  • data (pd.DataFrame) – Input dataframe.

  • file_path (Path) – Output feather file.

beak.experimental.io.get_filename_and_extension(file: Path) → Tuple[str, str][source]

Read filename and extension from a given file path.

Parameters:

file (Path) – The path to the file.

Returns:

A tuple containing the filename and extension.

Return type:

Tuple[str, str]

beak.experimental.io.load_dataset(file: Path | str, encoding_type: str = 'ISO-8859-1', nrows: int | None = None, **kwargs) → DataFrame[source]

Load a text-based dataset from disk.

Parameters:

  • file (Path) – Location of the file to be loaded.

  • encoding_type (str) – Text encoding of the input file. Defaults to “ISO-8859-1”.

  • nrows (int, optional) – Number of rows to be loaded. Defaults to None.

  • **kwargs – Additional keyword arguments to be passed to pd.read_csv().

Returns:

Table with loaded data.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

beak.experimental.io.load_feather(file_path: Path, columns: List | None = None, use_threads: bool = True, storage_options: dict | None = None, nrows: int | None = None) → DataFrame[source]

Load data from a feather file into a pandas DataFrame.

Parameters:

  • file_path (Path) – The path to the feather file.

  • columns (Optional[List], optional) – A list of column names to load. Defaults to None.

  • use_threads (bool, optional) – Whether to use multiple threads for reading. Defaults to True.

  • storage_options (Optional[dict], optional) – Additional options for storage. Defaults to None.

  • nrows (Optional[int], optional) – The number of rows to load. Defaults to None.

Returns:

The loaded data as a pandas DataFrame.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

beak.experimental.io.load_model(model: dict, folders: List[str | Path], file_extensions: List[str] | None = None, verbose: int = 0)[source]

Load model from dictionary and search for corresponding files.

Parameters:

  • model (dict) – The model dictionary containing evidence layers.

  • folders (Sequence[Path]) – The list of folders to search for files.

  • file_extensions (Sequence[str], optional) – The file extensions to consider. Defaults are [“.tif”, “.tiff”].

  • verbose (int, optional) – The verbosity level. Defaults to 1.

Raises:

  • ValueError – Raised if no valid selection is found.

  • ValueError – Raised if no files are found.

Returns:

A dictionary containing the loaded model with corresponding files. list: A list of all files found. Counter: A counter containing the number of files for each evidence layer.

Return type:

dict

beak.experimental.io.load_raster(file: str | Path, return_meta: bool = False) → DatasetReader | Tuple[DatasetReader, Dict][source]

Load a single raster file using rasterio.

Parameters:

  • file (Path) – The path to the raster file.

  • return_meta (bool) – If True, return the raster metadata along with the dataset.

  • False. (Defaults to)

Returns:

The opened raster dataset. Dict: The raster metadata.

Return type:

rasterio.io.DatasetReader

beak.experimental.io.load_rasters(folder: Path, extensions: Sequence[str] | None = None) → Tuple[Sequence[Path], Sequence[DatasetReader]][source]

Load raster files of a given type from a folder.

Parameters:

  • folder (Path) – The folder path where the raster files are located.

  • extensions (List[str]) – List of file extensions to consider. Defaults are [‘.tif’, ‘.tiff’].

Returns:

A tuple containing the list of file paths and the loaded raster datasets.

Return type:

Tuple[List[Path], List[rasterio.io.DatasetReader]]

beak.experimental.io.read_raster(raster: DatasetReader, replace_nan=False) → ndarray[source]

Read a raster single-band raster dataset.

Parameters:

  • raster (rasterio.io.DatasetReader) – The raster dataset to read.

  • replace_nan (bool) – Whether to replace the nodata value with NaN. Defaults to False.

Returns:

The raster data as a NumPy array.

Return type:

np.ndarray

beak.experimental.io.read_rasters(raster_list: List[DatasetReader]) → List[ndarray][source]

Read multiple rasters and return them as a list of NumPy arrays.

Parameters:

raster_list (List[rasterio.io.DatasetReader]) – A list of rasterio dataset readers.

Returns:

A list of NumPy arrays representing the read rasters.

Return type:

List[np.ndarray]

beak.experimental.io.save_geodataframe(dataframe: GeoDataFrame, path: str | Path, overwrite: bool = False)[source]

Save a GeoDataFrame to a file.

This function saves a GeoDataFrame to a file in either ESRI Shapefile or GeoPackage format, depending on the file extension provided in the output path.

Parameters:

  • dataframe (gpd.GeoDataFrame) – The GeoDataFrame to be saved.

  • path (Path) – The path to the output file. The file extension determines the format: - “.shp” for ESRI Shapefile - “.gpkg” for GeoPackage

  • overwrite (bool) – Whether to overwrite the file if it already exists. Defaults to False.

Returns:

None

beak.experimental.io.save_input_file_list(file_path: Path, file_list: Sequence[Path | str], only_existing: bool = True)[source]

Save a list of input files to a text file.

Parameters:

  • file_path (Path) – The path to the output text file.

  • file_list (List[Union[Path, str]]) – The list of input files to be saved.

Returns:

None

beak.experimental.io.save_raster(path: str | Path, array: ndarray, crs: CRS | None = None, height: int | None = None, width: int | None = None, nodata_value: Number | None = None, transform: Affine | None = None, dtype: str | dtype | None = None, metadata: Dict | None = None, compress_method: str | None = 'lzw', compress_num_threads: int | str | None = 'all_cpus', overwrite: bool = False)[source]

Save raster data to disk.

Parameters:

  • path (Path) – The file path where the raster will be saved.

  • array (np.ndarray) – The raster data as a NumPy array.

  • crs – (Optional[rasterio.crs.CRS]): The coordinate reference system of the raster.

  • height (Optional[int]) – The height (number of rows) of the raster.

  • width (Optional[int]) – The width (number of columns) of the raster.

  • nodata_value (Optional[np.number]) – The nodata value of the raster.

  • transform (affine.Affine) – The affine transformation matrix that maps pixel coordinates to CRS coordinates.

  • dtype (Optional[Union[str, np.dtype]]) – The data type of the raster. Either from class np.dtype or a string like “int8” or “float32”. Defaults to None.

  • metadata (Optional[Dict]) – Additional metadata to be included in the raster file. If provided, remaining metadata arguments will be ignored. Defaults to None.

  • compress_method (Optional[str]) – The compression method to use. Defaults to “lzw”.

  • compress_num_threads (Optional[Union[int, str]]) – The number of threads to use for compression. Defaults to “all_cpus”.

  • overwrite (bool) – Whether to overwrite the existing file. Defaults to False.

Returns:

None

Return type:

(None)

beak.experimental.io.spatial_filter(data: DataFrame, longitude_column: str | None = None, latitude_column: str | None = None, longitude_min: Number | None = None, longitude_max: Number | None = None, latitude_min: Number | None = None, latitude_max: Number | None = None) → DataFrame[source]

Filter data by spatial extent.

Parameters:

  • data (pd.DataFrame) – Input dataframe.

  • longitude_min (Number) – Minimum longitude.

  • longitude_max (Number) – Maximum longitude.

  • latitude_min (Number) – Minimum latitude.

  • latitude_max (Number) – Maximum latitude.

Returns:

Filtered dataframe.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame
